channel.title: Photo Feeds
channel.description: Combined feed of recent photo essays from The Atlantic, The Guardian, The Boston Globe, NYT, and Reuters.

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, England — Philippines’ Alexandra Eala returns to Poland’s Iga Swiatek during their third round match at Wimbledon. — Photograph: Henry Nicholls/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Paris, France — A model presents a creation by the designer Manish Malhotra as part of his haute couture fall/winter 2026-27 at Paris fashion week. — Photograph: Sarah Meyssonnier/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Paris, France — Coralie holds her pet parrot, Dali, up to a mist fountain to cool him down on the banks of the Seine. — Photograph: Tom Nicholson/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Calonge, Spain — A person points towards a wildfire near Calonge. Barcelona set a new heat record this week with temperatures of 40.5C, Spanish meteorologists said, while in France a 22-year-old firefighter died after tackling a blaze in the Alps, the French interior ministry reported. — Photograph: Bruna Casas/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Cercosa, Portugal — Firefighters battle the flames of the Vouzela wildfire. Raging infernos have laid waste to large areas of southern Europe in recent days, prompting the EU to scramble firefighters and water-bearing planes to help national services overwhelmed by simultaneous blazes. — Photograph: Paulo Cunha/EPA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Havana, Cuba — A street vendor uses a portable lamp to sell goods along the Malecón during a nationwide blackout. Cuba was already struggling to keep the lights on before Donald Trump imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. — Photograph: Adalberto Roque/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Disappointed fans watch the World Cup match between USA and Belgium at the National Mall. The buildup to the match had been tinged with controversy after Fifa suspended the red card the US striker Folarin Balogun was given in the last 32 against Bosnia and Herzegovina. — Photograph: Eric Lee/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Fireworks explode during the Great American State Fair on the National Mall. Donald Trump hailed the ‘unmatched achievement and unlimited potential’ of the US in a triumphalist address marking the country’s 250th anniversary. — Photograph: Rod Lamkey/AP

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — Children sit on the floor as Donald Trump speaks on the first day of trading for Trump Accounts in the Oval Office. — Photograph: Shawn Thew/UPI/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Washington DC, US — A commuter sits as members of the group Patriot Front ride the metro on the 250th anniversary of US independence on the Fourth of July. The neo-fascist hate group has gained visibility as those connected to white supremacy organisations have been widely embraced by the Trump administration. — Photograph: Cheney Orr/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — Nuns gather after accompanying a burial for earthquake victims in the aftermath of the 24 June earthquakes at La Esperanza cemetery. Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as the US-backed government’s inadequate response to the disaster before international teams arrived. — Photograph: Adriano Machado/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — People transport belongings inside a damaged building in the aftermath of the 24 June earthquakes. Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, defended her country’s emergency response to the twin earthquakes that have killed more than 3,000 people, vowing Venezuela would not descend into social unrest. — Photograph: Adriano Machado/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A resident waits for rescuers who are searching for her relative inside an apartment building hit during Russian missile and drone strikes. Volodymyr Zelenskyy has argued for Ukraine to be allowed to join Nato at its annual summit, saying it would be wrong to exclude a country that had built strong defences in its long struggle against the Russian invasion. — Photograph: Valentyn Ogirenko/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A firefighter works at the site of a Russian missile strike. Donald Trump told Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the Nato summit that Ukraine may be allowed to manufacture Patriot missile interceptors to counter Russian ballistic attacks. — Photograph: Gleb Garanich/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Kyiv, Ukraine — A woman holds her daughter after a Russian missile strike on a residential building. A wave of Russian missiles and drones struck across Ukraine this week, killing 21 people and heavily damaging apartment blocks and other buildings in an attack on the eve of a Nato summit in Turkey that has exposed widening gaps in Ukraine’s air defences. — Photograph: Genya Savilov/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Najaf, Iraq — Mourners carry the coffin of Iran’s slain supreme leader Ali Khamenei at the Imam Ali shrine. The scale of the six-day, five-city funeral in Iran and Iraq was conceived to relay political and religious messages of resistance to the rest of the world. — Photograph: Hussein Faleh/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City — A girl fills containers with water drawn from the leftovers of underground pipes at a camp for people displaced by war. Israel identified about 1,000 potential targets a day during the first two years of the wars in Gaza and Lebanon with its command and control system, according to a presentation by the country’s largest arms supplier, Elbit Systems. — Photograph: Omar Al-Qattaa/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] The week around the world in 20 pictures
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Gaza City — Palestinian football fans gather to watch the World Cup match between Argentina and Egypt. Mohamed al-Wahidi, a Palestinian aid worker who had organised screenings of World Cup matches in Gaza, was killed by an Israeli missile strike just before the match on Tuesday evening. — Photograph: Mohammed Skaik/TheNews2/Cover Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — A worker pushes a specially designed crate carrying the Bayeux tapestry into the British Museum. The arrival of the 11th-century artwork depicting the Norman conquest marked the first time it has returned to England in nearly 1,000 years. — Photograph: Richard A Brooks/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Hagetmau, France — The peloton passes spectators during the start of stage 7 of the Tour de France between Hagetmau and Bordeaux. Tadej Pogacar leads the standings board after his victory in stage 6 of the race. — Photograph: Anne-Christine Poujoulat/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Vulcano, Italy — People immerse themselves in the natural thermal mud pool of the island off Sicily. — Photograph: Marco Bertorello/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Konya, Turkey — An aerial view of the Selçuklu flower garden, where approximately 385,000 flowers of 84 varieties from 28 different species bloom across 11,500 sq metres. The garden attracts thousands of visitors each year. — Photograph: Serhat Cetinkaya/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Zoige, China — Tourists visit Flower Lake during the peak summer travel season in Sichuan province. Flower Lake is an important water conservation area in the upper reaches of the Yellow River. — Photograph: VCG/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
La Guaira, Venezuela — People displaced by last month’s double earthquake gather at a temporary shelter. The official death toll from the twin quakes passed ​3,810 this week as Venezuela’s interim president, Delcy Rodriguez, renewed calls ​for international sanctions on the country to be lifted to assist the recovery. — Photograph: Anadolu/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
El Dabaa, Egypt — Fans gather to welcome the Egypt men’s football team at El Alamein airport before an official reception hosted by President Abdel Fatah al-Sisi. Egypt were knocked out of the 2026 World Cup by Argentina on Tuesday. — Photograph: Mohamed Abdel Ghany/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chichester, UK — A Bugatti (L) and an Alfa Romeo P3 (Tipo B) are driven up the hill during the Goodwood Festival of Speed, which runs until Sunday. — Photograph: Simon Galloway/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Henley, UK — Visitors watch fireworks as the sun sets over the Thames during the Henley festival. The annual music and arts festival relaxed its black tie dress code for audience members on its opening day because of the heatwave. — Photograph: James Robinson

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Dunsden Green, UK — The sun rises over countryside in Oxfordshire at the beginning of another hot day. Temperatures of 35C or higher have now been recorded in May, June and July of the same year for the first time in the UK, the Met Office has said. — Photograph: Geoffrey Swaine/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Maidenhead, UK — A rower sculls on the Thames during the country’s third heatwave of 2026. The heatwave is to continue next week as forecasters confirm that last month was western Europe’s hottest June on record. — Photograph: Jonathan Brady/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Die, France — A wildfire burns in the hills above the south-east French town. Wildfires have burned through twice as much land in France so far this year as in the same period in 2025, according to officials. France is facing its third heatwave since May. — Photograph: Manon Cruz/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Almería, Spain — A firefighting helicopter dumps water over a wildfire in Los Gallardos. At least 12 people have been killed and 23 are unaccounted for after one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires broke out in the south-eastern province. — Photograph: Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Beijing, China — Two women carrying an umbrella walk in heavy rain in the Chinese capital. The city has issued its first amber rainstorm alert of the year and has evacuated nearly 100,000 residents. — Photograph: Maxim Shemetov/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Keelung, Taiwan — Statues outside the Dianji temple are covered with protective tarpaulins before the arrival of Typhoon Bavi, which has already triggered a deadly landslide in the Philippines. — Photograph: I-Hwa Cheng/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] A Tour de France picnic and Typhoon Bavi: photos of the day – Friday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Yilan, Taiwan — A fisher handles a rope near a fishing boat as waves crash over a breakwater before the arrival of Typhoon Bavi, which is forecast to be one of the strongest storms in decades. — Photograph: I-Hwa Cheng/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Jens Büttner / DPA / Getty — A dancer welcomes visitors to the airfield grounds at the Airbeat One electronic-music festival in Neustadt-Glewe, Germany, on July 9, 2026. About 200,000 electronic-music fans from more than 60 countries were expected to attend the festival.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Samuel de Roman / Anadolu / Getty — An aloitador holds a wild horse during the first curro in Sabucedo, Spain, on July 4, 2026. The Rapa das Bestas of Sabucedo is a festival held every year during the first week of July, where aloitadors immobilize and cut the manes and tails of wild horses.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Jens Büttner / DPA / Getty — A fish eagle, about five weeks old, is lifted out of its nest by a utility worker for banding in Neustadt-Glewe, Germany, on July 6, 2026. Bird experts are currently banding the chicks that hatched a few weeks ago, with support from the energy provider WeMag.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Temilade Adelaja / Reuters — An Atlantic puffin, seen during the 2026 annual census, monitoring the seabirds’ breeding population, on the Inner Farne Islands, Northumberland, England, on July 6, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Yuichi Yamazaki / AFP / Getty — A stuffed bear, (wearing a sign that reads “bear”), used as a target, stands in bushes during an emergency-response drill for bear and other wildlife intrusions, in Isehara, Kanagawa prefecture, Japan, on July 3, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Luis Cortes / Reuters — A Mexico fan reacts as they watch the Mexico versus England World Cup match on a big screen in Mexico City on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Sebastian Barros / AP — Fans watch the World Cup Round of 16 soccer match between Colombia and Switzerland on a terrace rooftop in Bogota, Colombia, on July 7, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Wang Wangwang / VCG / Getty — Fishermen work at a marine ranch in the waters of Sansha Town on July 4, 2026, in Xiapu county, Fujian province, China.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Tiziana Fabi / AFP / Getty — People wade along Rabbit Beach on the Italian island of Lampedusa, a protected nature reserve with limited daily visitor access to avoid over-tourism, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Konstantinos Tsakalidis / Bloomberg / Getty — A rescuer walks away from a burning factory during a wildfire near Oraiokastro, a suburb of Thessaloniki, Greece, on July 4, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Blanca Cruz / AFP / Getty — A model presents a creation by Iris Van Herpen for the Women Haute Couture Fall/Winter 2026–27 collection, as part of Paris Fashion Week, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Li Yalong / China News Service / Getty — A tourist wearing an astronaut suit takes part in a space-roaming experience at Alien Valley Geopark in Sunan Yugur autonomous county, Gansu province, China, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Ricardo Arduengo / Reuters — A dog rests as rescuers (not pictured) work at the site of a collapsed building in the aftermath of the June 24 earthquakes in La Guaira, Venezuela, on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Adriano Machado / Reuters — A man uses an angle grinder at the site of a collapsed building in the aftermath of the June 24 earthquakes in La Guaira, Venezuela, on July 4, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Majid Saeedi / Getty — A person kicks a large banner depicting U.S. President Trump with a target on his head during funeral ceremonies for Iran’s slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, in Tehran on July 5, 2026. Khamenei and members of his family were killed on February 28 during U.S.-Israeli strikes.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Wakil Kohsar / AFP / Getty — Water is sprayed to cool off people gathering to mourn Iran’s slain supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei at the Grand Mosalla on the second day of funeral ceremonies in Tehran, on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Ding Minghua / VCG / Getty — Heavy rainfall drops from the sky above Qianshan, Jiangxi province, China, on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Abdul Saboor / Reuters — People watch as a firefighting plane makes a drop, battling a fire near the A9 motorway near Béziers, France, on July 3, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Huang Zhengwei / VCG / Getty — Tourists watch muddy water rushing out of the Xiaolangdi Dam on the Yellow River during a water-sediment regulation operation in Luoyang, Henan province, China, on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Stefan Sauer / DPA / Getty — Participants line up at the water’s edge in the harbor of Parow near Stralsund, Germany, at the start of the International Sound Swim on July 4, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Miguel Oses / AP — Revelers celebrate as the traditional rocket marking the start of the San Fermín festival launches, kicking off nine days of uninterrupted festivities in Pamplona, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Brett Davis / Imagn Images / Reuters — Argentina’s Lionel Messi is thrown in the air in celebration by teammates after a match as Argentina qualified for the quarterfinals of the World Cup by defeating Egypt on July 7, 2026, in Atlanta, Georgia.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Jose Jordan / AFP / Getty — A bull jumps into the sea after chasing runners during the traditional Bous a la Mar (“Bulls in the Sea”) at Denia’s harbor near Alicante, Spain, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Jorge Guerrero / AFP / Getty — Participants run ahead of bulls from the Fuente Ymbro bull ranch during the first running of the bulls of the San Fermín festival in Pamplona, northern Spain, on July 7, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Valery Hache / AFP / Getty — People stand in front of a coffee shop during a heat wave in the French Riviera city of Nice on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Qu Honglun / China News Service / Getty — A visitor poses while exploring in Yanzi Cave in Qiannan Bouyei and Miao autonomous prefecture, Guizhou Province, China, on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Nathan Howard / Reuters — Lightning strikes behind the illuminated Washington Monument as people gather to watch the fireworks during Freedom 250’s Salute to America as the United States celebrated its 250th anniversary on Independence Day, in Washington, D.C., on July 5, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos of the Week: Rabbit Beach, Puffin Census, Wild Horses
item.description:
Mike Blake / Reuters — Marseel Bahnan, originally from Iraq, poses with her face painted in the colors of the U.S. flag during a World Cup watch party in San Diego, California, as the United States played against Belgium on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [Reuters — The Wider Image] World Cup Brings Fans Joy Torment Lifetime Memories
item.description:
Soccer Football - FIFA World Cup 2026 - Mexico v South Africa - Fans gather in Mexico City - Zocalo, Mexico City, Mexico - June 11, 2026 - General view of a giant FIFA World Cup 2026 ball as the Mexico fans watch the match REUTERS/Marian Carrasquero

Source: Reuters — The Wider Image

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
London, UK — King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive for a visit to Penguin Beach to celebrate the 200th anniversary of the Zoological Society of London at London zoo in Regent’s Park. — Photograph: Jonathan Brady/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Florida, US — A SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket carrying 29 Starlink satellites lifts off from Cape Canaveral. — Photograph: Jennifer Briggs/Zuma/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Pau, France — The Tour de France peloton passes through landscape before stage 6, a 186.2km stage from Pau to Gavarnie-Gèdre. — Photograph: Tim de Waele/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Edinburgh, UK — Firefighters at the scene of a blaze at a building on Princes Street. Emergency services were called to the fire at the old Debenhams building at 2.52am on Thursday. — Photograph: Jane Barlow/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New Taipei City, Taiwan — Fishing boats secured at Shen’ao harbour before the arrival of Typhoon Bavi. — Photograph: I-Hwa Cheng/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Chennai, India — A shaded footpath in the city. — Photograph: R Satish Babu/AFP/Getty

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Die, France — A wildfire has been burning mountain vegetation for several days in the Drome department during a heatwave affecting a large part of the country. — Photograph: Manon Cruz/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
New York City, US — A girl runs through sprinklers. — Photograph: Jordan Tovin/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Venice, Italy — The French artist James Colomina’s unauthorised artwork Nothing Serious, depicting a child drifting on a life buoy with a teddy bear, floats on the Rio di San Felice, in the Cannaregio district. — Photograph: Manuel Silvestri/Reuters

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Worcester, UK — Swans on the River Severn. Temperatures are set to continue to rise across the UK throughout the week and more regions are likely to reach heatwave thresholds. — Photograph: David Davies/PA

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Swans in Worcester and floating art in Venice: photos of the day – Thursday

item.description:

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Chris McGrath / Getty — Emergency-service personnel work to repair the roof of the Dormition Cathedral of the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra after it was burned and damaged by Russian strikes on June 16, 2026, in Kyiv, Ukraine.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Tetiana Dzhafarova / AFP / Getty — A woman kneels next to a makeshift memorial near a residential building that was damaged during a Russian air attack on July 3, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
lina Smutko / Reuters — Iryna Plekhova, a resident who survived the previous day’s Russian missile strikes, carries some of her damaged personal belongings, including a Ukrainian coat of arms carved out of wood by her father-in-law, from her burned apartment on July 3, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Paula Bronstein / Getty — People watch as emergency workers clear debris from the scene of an overnight Russian missile and drone attack on Kyiv that killed at least 14 people, on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — Rescuers work at the site of a damaged apartment building in Kyiv on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Danylo Antoniuk / AP — Liudmyla Tsapkova sits in her damaged apartment after a Russian missile attack in Kyiv on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Danylo Antoniuk / AP — Emergency-services personnel work to extinguish a fire following Russian missile attacks in Kyiv on July 6, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — Firefighters work to extinguish a fire at the Dormition Cathedral of the Kyiv Pechersk Lavra, which was hit during Russian missile and drone strikes on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Marianna Kotyk / Reuters — Volunteers collect glass shards in a building at the Oleksandr Dovzhenko National Film Studio in Kyiv on June 19, 2026. The studio sustained significant damage during a large-scale missile and drone strike several days earlier. The costume shop, which housed the largest and oldest costume collection in Ukraine, was heavily affected.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Libkos / Getty — An aerial view shows the scale of destruction, including burned-down houses and damaged streets, in Vyshneve, on the outskirts of Kyiv, following a Russian night attack on July 6, 2026. The attack caused secondary detonations for more than three hours, destroying or heavily damaging hundreds of homes and forcing hundreds of residents to evacuate.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Gleb Garanich / Reuters — An explosion over the city, seen during a Russian missile and drone strike, on July 6, 2026

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Serhii Okunev / AFP / Getty — People shelter in a Kyiv metro station during a Russian missile strike on June 15, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Evgeniy Maloletka / AP — A Ukrainian rocket intercepts a Russian ballistic missile during a Russian attack on Kyiv on June 25, 2026. Recent shortages of U.S.-made interceptors have allowed more Russian ballistic missiles to get through, striking their targets.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Valentyn Ogirenko / Reuters — A woman cries while holding a child near the site of a damaged apartment building on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:
Roman Pilipey / AFP / Getty — A person walks next a residential building damaged from a Russian air attack on the Ukrainian capital on July 2, 2026.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Kostiantyn Liberov / Libkos / Getty — Rescue workers operate at a destroyed section of a residential building after an explosion caused by a Russian strike on July 2, 2026.

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Kostiantyn Liberov / Libkos / Getty — A panoramic view of Kyiv shows smoke from a large fire spreading over the city following an overnight Russian missile and drone attack on July 2, 2026. Russia’s overnight attack on the capital killed at least 31 people and injured 100 others.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.title: [The Atlantic — Photography] Photos: Russia’s Constant Bombardment of Kyiv
item.description:
Danylo Antoniuk / AP — A woman looks up at a burning apartment building after a Russian missile attack in Kyiv, Ukraine, on July 2, 2026. Russian missiles have hit sites across the country, but Kyiv’s infrastructure and residential areas are especially frequent targets.

Source: The Atlantic — Photography

item.description:
Havana, Cuba — A man looks at his phone in Havana during a nationwide blackout on Monday night. Cuba, which suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, was already struggling to keep the lights on before Trump imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power plants. — Photograph: Yamil Lage/AFP/Getty Images

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day

item.title: [The Guardian — Photos of the Day] Pamplona revellers and a Nato summit: photos of the day – Tuesday
item.source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
item.description:
Pamplona, Spain — People take part in the San Fermin festival’s traditional encierro (bull run). The bull-running fiesta is held annually from 6 to 14 July in commemoration of the city’s patron saint. Visitors from around the world attend the festival, and many participate in the highlight event, in which they attempt to outrun the bulls along a route through the narrow streets of the old city. — Photograph: Rubén Albarrán/ZUMA Press Wire/Shutterstock

Source: The Guardian — Photos of the Day
